The sale price was determined through a structured approach that considers several key factors, including market conditions, competitive positioning, and strategic objectives. This methodology ensures that the sale price reflects both the intrinsic value of the Monad network and broader market dynamics. The pricing strategy aims to [...] herein. At the start of Monad Public Mainnet, 50.6B tokens (50.6%) will be locked. Locked tokens cannot be staked. The intent of this is twofold: to ensure that staking rewards initially contribute to the public circulation of MON and to avoid an initial concentration of rewards among those individuals (team members and investors) involved directly with the project. evidences.title: Monad Token Debuts on Coinbase at $3.9 Billion Fully Diluted ...
evidences.content: Monad’s native token MON successfully launched on Coinbase with a $3.9 billion fully diluted valuation (FDV), marking a significant milestone in the cryptocurrency market. The Layer-1 blockchain project completed its initial token offering on Coinbase’s new token sale platform before transitioning to public trading. The token sale exceeded expectations by raising $269 million from more than 85,800 participants across 70+ countries. This impressive participation level demonstrates strong investor confidence in Monad’s vision and technology, despite initial concerns about the pace of the sale. evidences.title: Monad Raises 269 Million From Token Sale, Mainnet Launch Set
evidences.content: #### #### Funding and Token Sale: What’s Going On Monad has completed its token sale on Coinbase, raising $269 million from over 85,000 participants. The Ethereum-compatible layer-1 blockchain exceeded its initial fundraising goal of $187 million. The public token sale itself offers 7.5 billion MON (7.5 % of total supply) at $0.025 per token, implying a fully diluted valuation of roughly $2.5 billion if fully subscribed. The sale is being hosted on Coinbase’s newly launched regulated token-launch platform, accessible in more than 80 countries including the U.S. Participation rules include a minimum bid of $100, a maximum bid of $100,000, and a special allocation algorithm that prioritizes smaller bids to encourage broad retail access. [...] At launch day: Airdrop recipients (nearly 225,000 users) will receive allocations. Major wallets and infrastructure providers will support MON token listing and staking. Exchanges have confirmed day-one trading including major platforms. Validator network setup is expected to begin with dozens or hundreds of operators entering. #### #### The Layer-1 Race Heats Up Monad’s token sale and launch combination matter for several reasons: ##### Inst...

evidences.title: Coinbase's Historic First Token Sale Smashes Records with $269 ...
evidences.content: Coinbase has confirmed that it will distribute allocations to successful participants shortly, enabling investors to begin using their newly acquired MON tokens. The Coinbase exchange gave no specific timeline for the distribution but showed that details would follow soon after the sale’s conclusion. ## Understanding the Monad Ecosystem The Monad project has both a Layer 1 blockchain platform and a blockchain-native gaming ecosystem. As an independent blockchain, Monad is unique in its advanced technical design in the name of scalability and performance. The platform uses parallel execution as well as an optimized consensus algorithm to address blockchain limitations that plagued earlier generations of cryptocurrency networks. [...] logo logo # Coinbase’s Historic First Token Sale Smashes Records with $269 Million Raised for Monad TokenFi Monad Coinbase main3 Coinbase has successfully carried out their first-ever token sale, which is a large milestone for the cryptocurrency exchange, and was able to raise $269 million for the $MON token. The sale of the $MON token from the Monad blockchain attracted investors from all over the world and it had reached over 43 percent of its targ...

evidences.title: This Week in Digital Assets: Monad Launches Public Sale ...
evidences.content: This week, the Monad public token sale on Coinbase has become one of the tests so far of whether large, high fully diluted valuation (FDV) L1/L2 launches still command investor interest in a more regulated and more skeptical market. As of writing, the sale has raised a little almost $150 million, roughly 80% of its $187.5 million target at $0.025 per MON, with about two and a half days still to run and 7.5% of the 100 billion token supply on offer. We expect the sale to end up oversubscribed, but the order of magnitude is noticeably different from the recent L2 MegaETH public sale that targeted $50 million and drew around $1.4 billion of bids, roughly 28 times the goal. Token launches are clearly back in 2025, yet we see shifting investor behavior. The market is still willing to fund [...] For Coinbase, this sale is also a strategic move. The firm now has a mechanism to capture primary issuance economics and build deeper listing liquidity. Coinbase is planning to run around one token sale per month on this platform, which creates a predictable, programmatic pipeline of new assets for its spot markets and for potential staking and other products down the line. If we begin to see...

evidences.content: ### What's the Take? Coinbase has well and truly jumped the shark. Although Coinbase appears convinced that its token sales will not run afoul of U.S. regulators, it is the first time the exchange is conducting a direct token sale that is available to retail American users. Primary digital asset offerings – such as the MON token sale – may qualify as securities under the legal precedent established by SEC v. evidences.title: The ideal way to launch – introducing token sales on ...
evidences.content: Coinbase is providing access for the retail community across most regions globally at launch, with plans to expand in the future. For the first time since 2018, users in the United States will now be able to widely participate – a huge win for the American cryptoeconomy. We are hosting the first token sale from November 17-22; follow @Coinbase on X for more details. ### A New Standard for Token Sales Unlike "first-come, first-served" sales that fail to reach a project’s real community, our token sale design prioritizes the many over the few. Here’s how it works: [...] We will be hosting approximately one token sale per month, to ensure each sale has the full support and attention of our platform and community, while maintaining the highest standards. In the coming months, we plan to add features to our token sales platform including limit orders and higher allocations for issuers' target user bases. This is just the beginning. With Coinbase, we’re redefining how tokens reach real users — building a fairer, more sustainable future for crypto from the ground up. Assets that launch on Coinbase token sales will be added to the Coinbase listings roadmap. To read more about our first...

evidences.title: A Simple Analysis of MON Coin - Binance
evidences.content: Project Financing History According to the sales documents disclosed by the project, Monad's cumulative financing situation is as follows: Pre-Seed: June to December 2022, raised $19.6 million Seed: January to March 2024, raised $22.6 million Series A: March to August 2024, raised $220.5 million Total Cumulative Financing Amount: The total amount raised in the above three rounds is approximately $262.7 million. In addition, the Monad Foundation also received a $90 million donation from its core development entity, Category Labs, in 2024, which is included in the above total financing amount to cover operational expenses for 2024-2026. Investor Portion in Token Distribution In the total supply of MON tokens, a specific portion is reserved for early investors: [...] Investor Allocation Ratio: 19.7% of the initial total supply (10 billion tokens), which is 1.97 billion MON. Token Lockup: All tokens allocated to investors will be locked for at least one year from the mainnet launch (November 2025), and will gradually be unlocked according to the established schedule, expected to be fully released before the fourth quarter of 2029. Additionally Project Development Entity: The core de...

evidences.title: Monad public sale total commitments: More than $1B, bet - KuCoin
evidences.content: Monad public sale total commitments: More than $1B, bet: NO at 58c. Easiest would be to compare it with MegaETH which raised $1,4b at a $1b val, and we have to remember that the valuation is higher, and that the sentiment was better at that time of MegaETH + that the whole Europe is banned from this sale. So far $87m has been raised, and the goal is $187.5m. It will definitely be oversubscribed, but the question is just by how much. The ICO price is closer to the pre-market price, and I think people will be more skeptical about apeing hard. On the bull side for Monad, though they have Coinbase in their back + the sale lasts for 5 more days. Absolutely not a sure bet at all, but IMO it could be worth a try. evidences.title: Monad's $140M Public Sale: A Launchpad for High-Performance ...
evidences.content: Critics argue that the sale's structure-fixed-price, non-first-come, first-served-reduced urgency for early participation. Additionally, the token allocation model has drawn scrutiny: 50.6% of the total 100 billion MON supply is locked at launch, with 27% reserved for the team and 19.7% for investors. Such allocations, coupled with 20% of funding coming from venture capitalists, raise concerns about centralization and long-term sell pressure. [...] Monad's public sale began with a surge of enthusiasm, raising $43 million in the first 23 minutes-23% of the 7.5 billion MON tokens available. However, this initial fervor quickly waned, with the sale only reaching 48% subscription by the end of the first week, leaving over $100 million in tokens unallocated. This tepid response contrasts sharply with the recent oversubscribed MegaETH token sale, which closed at a $27.8 billion fully diluted valuation (FDV) in October 2025. [...] Yet, institutional credibility is a mitigating factor. Anchorage Digital's role as the preferred custodian for MON, offering secure staking and custody via its Porto wallet, signals institutional trust. Furthermore, the sale's $0.025 price point implies a $2....

evidences.title: Monad Launches Mainnet, MON Trades Near ICO Price - "The Defiant"
evidences.content: the-defiant Despite the slow start, Monad’s ICO ended up raising roughly $269 million in total commitments from about 85,800 participants in the debut token sale on Coinbase’s new platform, after the CEX acquired crypto fundraising platform Echo in October. Demand exceeded the available allocation by roughly $82 million, making the sale about 1.43x oversubscribed on commitments. Notably, the number of participants is far more than recent ICOs, and more than double the participants in MegaETH’s recent token sale. Founded in 2022 by Keone Hon, James Hunsaker and Eunice Giarta, Monad was pitched as an EVM compatible Layer 1 that prioritizes parallel execution and a purpose built state database to deliver high throughput and low latency. [...] The Monad public token sale offered roughly 7.5% of the 100 billion total token supply at $0.025, an allocation that implied about $187.5 million in available public funding. Some in the community earlier expressed concern that only 3.3% of the total MON supply is set aside to be airdropped, meaning, when combined with the ICO allocation, only around 11% of the total supply will be available to the average retail investor. the-defiant [...] Ad...

evidences.title: [PDF] Form-10K-2025.pdf
evidences.content: Subject to jurisdiction, we support eight staking assets through our platform for consumers as of December 31, 2025: Cardano (ADA), Avalanche (AVAX), Cosmos (ATOM), Polkadot (DOT), Ethereum (ETH), MATIC (POL), Solana (SOL), and Tezos (XTZ). As of December 31, 2025, approximately $7.5 billion worth of these assets were held on behalf of individual consumers staked through our platform, as measured in U.S. dollar equivalents. [...] For our institutional customers, our staking process varies by customer. In addition to operating our own validator nodes to provide staking services, we also provide institutional customers access to validator nodes operated by third-party service providers. Institutional customers receive rewards directly from the protocol. The fees we charge to institutional customers depend on the customer agreement and the selected service. As of December 31, 2025, over $15.2 billion worth of assets were staked by institutional customers through Coinbase Prime, as adjusted to USD. We also operate a cbETH token wrapping service. cbETH is an Ethereum-based “wrapped staking token” that represents ownership of ETH staked through our platform. [...] 6 Table of Contents...

evidences.content: # Monad's public sale on Coinbase has reached a subscription target of 188 million USD, and the sale will end on November 23 at 10:00. | Foresight_News on Binance Square Image 1: Binance Square Login Sign Up HomeTrending ArticlesNotificationProfileNewsBookmarksChatsHistoryCreator CenterSettings Article # Monad's public sale on Coinbase has reached a subscription target of 188 million USD, and the sale will end on November 23 at 10:00. Image 2 Image 3 Foresight_News Nov 22, 2025 Follow View Hidden Replies Report Block User [...] Follow View Hidden Replies Report Block User Foresight News: The public sale of Monad tokens has reached the subscription target of 188 million USDC, with 13 hours remaining until the sale ends. The price per token is 0.025 USDC. Additionally, the MON-USDC perpetual contract on Hyperliquid is currently reported at approximately 0.03 USD. Foresight News: Previously reported, the public sale of Monad will end on November 23 at 10:00, with a total available amount of 188 million USDC. evidences.title: Coinbase Opens a New Chapter for Token Sales - NCFA Canada
evidences.content: Coinbase has confirmed that Monad (MON) will be the first project to use the new platform, with its token sale scheduled for November 17–22, 2025. A key test of how a compliant, exchange led distribution can work in practice. The design directly addresses problems seen during the 2017–2018 ICO boom, when many projects raised funds without meeting disclosure or compliance standards. Coinbase’s structured, transparent model supports fair access for users while maintaining clear accountability for issuers. ## Then and Now: Token Sales Compared
